prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The Toronto municipal election held on October 27, 2014, resulted in the election of a mayor and 44 city councillors, as well as school trustees to the Toronto District School Board, Toronto Catholic District School Board, Conseil scolaire de district du Centre-Sud-Ouest and Conseil scolaire de district Centre-Sud. The election took place in tandem with those of the other municipalities in Ontario.
B. The 2014 Toronto municipal election was held on October 27, 2014, to elect a mayor and 44 city councillors in Toronto, Ontario, Canada. In addition, school trustees were elected to the Toronto District School Board, Toronto Catholic District School Board, Conseil scolaire de district du Centre-Sud-Ouest and Conseil scolaire de district catholique Centre-Sud. The election was held in conjunction with those held in other municipalities in the province of Ontario. Candidate registration opened on January 2, 2014, and closed on September 12, 2014, at 2pm EST.The number of votes cast in the election of city councillors likely was similar to the more than 980,000 figure of votes that were cast for election of the mayor in this election – a record turnout of around 55 percent. Christin Carmichael
C. On October 27, 2014, residents of Toronto, Ontario, Canada participated in the 2014 Toronto municipal election. This electoral event aimed to choose a new mayor along with 44 city councillors for the municipality. Beyond these primary roles, the election also involved the selection of school trustees for several educational boards: the Toronto District School Board, the Toronto Catholic District School Board, the Conseil scolaire de district du Centre-Sud-Ouest, and the Conseil scolaire de district catholique Centre-Sud. This election was synchronized with other municipal elections occurring throughout the province of Ontario on the same date. The period for candidates to register began on January 2, 2014, and concluded precisely at 2pm EST on September 12, 2014. It is probable that the total number of votes submitted for the city councillor elections was comparable to the over 980,000 votes recorded for the mayoral election in this same event. This figure represented a historic voter turnout, approximately 55 percent of eligible voters. Christin Carmichael

Answer:

options.A: The Toronto municipal election held on October 27, 2014, resulted in the election of a mayor and 44 city councillors, as well as school trustees to the Toronto District School Board, Toronto Catholic District School Board, Conseil scolaire de district du Centre-Sud-Ouest and Conseil scolaire de district Centre-Sud. The election took place in tandem with those of the other municipalities in Ontario.
options.B: The 2014 Toronto municipal election was held on October 27, 2014, to elect a mayor and 44 city councillors in Toronto, Ontario, Canada. In addition, school trustees were elected to the Toronto District School Board, Toronto Catholic District School Board, Conseil scolaire de district du Centre-Sud-Ouest and Conseil scolaire de district catholique Centre-Sud. The election was held in conjunction with those held in other municipalities in the province of Ontario. Candidate registration opened on January 2, 2014, and closed on September 12, 2014, at 2pm EST.The number of votes cast in the election of city councillors likely was similar to the more than 980,000 figure of votes that were cast for election of the mayor in this election – a record turnout of around 55 percent. Christin Carmichael
options.C: On October 27, 2014, residents of Toronto, Ontario, Canada participated in the 2014 Toronto municipal election. This electoral event aimed to choose a new mayor along with 44 city councillors for the municipality. Beyond these primary roles, the election also involved the selection of school trustees for several educational boards: the Toronto District School Board, the Toronto Catholic District School Board, the Conseil scolaire de district du Centre-Sud-Ouest, and the Conseil scolaire de district catholique Centre-Sud. This election was synchronized with other municipal elections occurring throughout the province of Ontario on the same date. The period for candidates to register began on January 2, 2014, and concluded precisely at 2pm EST on September 12, 2014. It is probable that the total number of votes submitted for the city councillor elections was comparable to the over 980,000 votes recorded for the mayoral election in this same event. This figure represented a historic voter turnout, approximately 55 percent of eligible voters. Christin Carmichael
